textPlain: This post reminds me of something.During my first Introduction to Programming course at university, I was taught Java. One thing that I found very troubling is that it wasn't easy, or possible in many cases, to change the programming language. Sure, you can write new functions, but I can't create a new keyword for an if-statement. I also remember the TA saying "why would you want that?"15 years later,  I still have this issue a bit, except I made my peace with it. It is what it is (well... except for Lisp, Smalltalk and similar languages - which is in part why I have worked for a company that professionally programmed in Pharo - I remember hacking a very crude way for runtime type checking in Pharo [1]).I'm not a Ruby programmer, all I know is that Ruby has some things that are identical to Smalltalk. But my question to the author would be: if you long for things like keyword arguments, type hints and namespaces why don't you program it in yourself?Or is that really hard, like most other languages?[1] https://youtu.be/FeFrt-kdvms?si=vlFPIkGuVceztVuW&t=2678
